text.title: Caching Strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped caching-strategy work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: All cached keys expire at the same minute; source DB sees a thundering herd; p95 spikes from 30ms to 4s.
antipattern.root-cause: TTL set without jitter; populated in a single batch.
antipattern.fix: Add ±20% jitter on TTL; stagger initial population. See rule ttl-with-jitter.

antipattern.symptom: Write commits to DB; cache returns stale value for the next 5 minutes; user sees old data after editing.
antipattern.root-cause: No invalidation; relied on TTL-only with 5-minute TTL.
antipattern.fix: Switch to pubsub-event OR write-through; document the contract. See rule invalidation-contract.

antipattern.symptom: Popular key expires; 200 concurrent requests all miss; DB takes 200 identical queries in 50ms.
antipattern.root-cause: No single-flight wrapper around the miss path.
antipattern.fix: Wrap miss-handler with singleflight (Go) / coalesce (Node) / lock-then-fill (Python). See rule single-flight-on-miss.

antipattern.symptom: Cache misconfiguration drops hit-rate to 5%; runs unnoticed for 4 days; bill triples on DB.
antipattern.fix: Export hit_rate metric; alert below hit_rate_target. See rule hit-rate-floor.
